name: Vardenafil Healthy-Adult Cognition Trial Extract
population_or_context: Healthy young adults selected from volunteers based on memory-screening performance.
sample_size: Not extracted from abstract; full text not checked.
methods: Healthy adults were orally treated with vardenafil 10-20 mg or placebo and tested on memory, executive functioning, EEG, reaction time, and complaint questionnaires.
population_profile: Healthy young adults; closer population directness than CSVD trials but not tadalafil and not specifically men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: No tadalafil exposure; adjacent PDE5-inhibitor evidence using vardenafil 10-20 mg versus placebo.
indication_context: Experimental healthy-volunteer cognitive-performance study with a non-tadalafil PDE5 inhibitor.
study_design: Behavioral EEG placebo-comparator study in healthy adults; exact randomization/blinding details require full-text confirmation.
safety_findings: The PubMed abstract reports headache and feeling weak more often after vardenafil treatment.
directness_to_target_population: Adjacent only: healthy-adult human cognition evidence, but the drug was vardenafil rather than tadalafil and the population was not specifically mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Non-tadalafil evidence; abstract-only extraction; full design and sample details need full-text verification before high-confidence use.
